author: usmannasir
message: Merge v2.4.8 into stable: CyberPanel-OLS stack 2.5.1 / 2.7.5 / 2.5.1 rollout

author: usmannasir
message:
Roll out CyberPanel-OLS stack 2.5.1 (core) / 2.7.5 (module) / 2.5.1 (modsec)

Fixes the 4xx segfault / Cloudflare 520 storm caused by cyberpanel_ols
2.7.0-2.7.3 crashing the worker on requests whose Host matched no vhost.
Core 2.5.1 hardens HttpReq::getDocRoot against NULL vhost so no module
can retrigger this crash class.

- Pin new sha256 checksums for all three artifacts on rhel8/rhel9/ubuntu
- Skip the custom overlay on any Ubuntu < 22.04 (artifact needs GLIBC 2.34)
- Full lsws stop/start around the binary swap; remove targets before copy
  (avoids ETXTBSY); never leave lsws stopped on a failure path
- Back up and restore the module and mod_security in rollback, not just
  the core binary
- Changelog: support notes for restoring the emergency module-block
  removal, ls_enabled semantics, and banned module builds

author: usmannasir
message:
Re-surface SSL on the site management page as its own tab

The May 29 tab-ification grouped the site management page into Overview /
Domains / Logs / Config / Files / Apps. SSL had no tab of its own -- the
"Add SSL" action and the paste-your-own-cert form were folded into the Config
tab with no SSL label, and the load script hides every non-active panel, so
users reported "the SSL menu disappeared after updating to 2.4.8"
(community thread 62145).

Add a dedicated SSL tab:
- new "SSL" entry in the tab bar and in the panel allow-list
- move the existing Add SSL action + own-cert form out of Config into the SSL
  panel (same websitePages controller scope, bindings unchanged)
- add an "Issue / Renew SSL" shortcut to the Manage SSL page (v2ManageSSL) so
  Let's Encrypt issuance is reachable from the site page

Template-only; no static asset or JS change.

author: usmannasir
message:
Fix remote transfer regression: status stuck on "Just started..", failed sends silently dropped

The transfer-status hardening switched FetchRemoteTransferStatus and
cancelRemoteTransfer to read /home/backup/transfer-* directly with open()/
os.kill(). Those paths are created chmod 600 and owned by root by the backup
process, so a non-root web worker hits PermissionError, which the bare
except swallowed -> the UI polled "Just started.." forever and cancel/cleanup
silently no-op'd. Restore reading/killing/removing through the privileged
executioner while keeping the new path-traversal validation (validated numeric
transfer dir, shlex.quote'd).

remoteTransferUtilities.sendBackup:
- default to SSH port 22 when /home/cyberpanel/remote_port is absent instead of
  failing the whole send (no manual workaround file required)
- make the scp exit code authoritative: only delete the local backup after a
  confirmed transfer, and log a clear [5010] failure (with the local copy kept)
  otherwise
- log send errors into the transfer backup_log so the UI surfaces them

backupProcess: tolerate a missing remote_port file during cleanup.

author: usmannasir
message: Merge v2.4.8 into stable (security fixes: GHSA-p9j3-wrpf-p6w3 + audit)

author: usmannasir
message:
Security: fix command injection, SQLi, path traversal, privilege escalation; crypto hardening

Addresses GHSA-p9j3-wrpf-p6w3 (authenticated RCE via changePHP) plus a broader
security audit. All changes are backward compatible with existing installs:
legitimate input passes through unchanged, modern installs keep reading
SECRET_KEY from .env, and legacy password hashes still verify.

Command injection:
- shlex.quote phpSelection/phpVersion/path/websiteOwner/package in website.py
  (changePHP, submitWebsiteCreation, submitDomainCreation, saveWebsiteChanges,
  switchServer) instead of escapable single-quote wrapping
- validate firewall rule proto/port/ip; quote deleteSSHKey/modifyPorts/saveSSHConfigs
- write Cloudflare ACME key via shlex.quote instead of raw shell echo
- quote cloudAPI SubmitS3BackupRestore args

SQL injection:
- backtick-quote db/table identifiers in mysqlUtilities table browser
  (fetchTables/deleteTable/fetchTableData/fetchStructure)
- validate username/host in databaseManager.allowRemoteIP

Path traversal / arbitrary file operations:
- allowlist log files in serverLogs.clearLogFile
- reject traversal/absolute paths in IncBackups.remove_destination and backup restore
- use realpath-based pathInside() consistently across filemanager operations

Authorization:
- changeACLFunc/submitUserCreation: require ownership and block non-admins from
  granting/creating admin-level ACLs

Crypto and secrets:
- remove hardcoded SECRET_KEY fallback (env -> persistent key file -> generate once)
- bcrypt for new password hashes; legacy salted SHA-256 still verified (no lockout)
- escape WordPress site data to prevent stored XSS in WPsitesList
- use secrets instead of random for password/token generation

author: usmannasir
message:
Force certificate reissue on manual SSL issue button (#1814)

A manual Issue/Reissue SSL click reported success but did not actually
reissue when a valid certificate already existed: the issuance backends
skip when CheckIfSSLNeedsToBeIssued / the existing-cert check says the
cert is still valid. That skip is correct for automated renewal (avoids
Let's Encrypt rate limits) but wrong for an explicit user reissue.

Thread a forceIssue flag from both manual endpoints (issueSSL and
issueSSLv2) down through the issuance chain to bypass the skip. All other
callers (auto-renewal, domain/alias/child/hostname/mail creation) keep the
default forceIssue=False and are unchanged.

author: usmannasir
message:
Stop secMiddleware from blocking valid webmail content (#1813)

Webmail input (message bodies, subjects, recipients, search queries,
sieve rules, contacts) is free-form text handled entirely through Python
IMAP/SMTP/Sieve libraries and never reaches a shell, so the shell-injection
character checks only produced false positives (e.g. an email reply
containing ; | $ or backticks). Skip input validation for /webmail/
requests while still applying the response security headers.

author: usmannasir
message:
Re-link Modify Website (and owner transfer) in the new design (#1816)

After the 2.4.8 UI redesign the Modify Website page (/websites/modifyWebsite)
was orphaned - it was only linked from the old home tiles, so users could
no longer find where to change a website's package, owner, email or PHP
version (owner transfer lives on this same page). The page itself already
extends the new base template; it just needed re-linking.

- listWebsites.html: add a 'Modify' action to each site row (guarded by
  the modifyWebsite ACL), alongside Manage/Settings/Filemanager.
- siteWorkspace.html: add a 'Modify Website' tile to the Advanced tab,
  sub-labelled 'Change package, owner, email & PHP' so ownership transfer
  is discoverable.

Both use the existing cp-tile / row-actions markup so they match the new
design language.

author: usmannasir
message:
Re-enable cyberpanel_ols module when found disabled (ls_enabled 0)

configureCustomModule() only added the module block when absent; if the
block already existed with 'ls_enabled 0' it was left disabled, silently
turning off LSCache and all .htaccess features. Detect that case in both
the install and upgrade paths and flip it back to 'ls_enabled 1' (with a
config backup), scoped to the cyberpanel_ols block so other modules are
untouched.

author: usmannasir
message:
Harden custom OLS install/upgrade: SHA256 verify, GLIBC pre-check, install-path health-check + rollback

Add the prevention measures the OLS deployment guide recommended but
that were missing from both the install and upgrade paths:

- SHA256 verification: each downloaded artifact (OLS binary, cyberpanel_ols
  module, mod_security) is checked against a hash pinned in the per-platform
  config dict before it is installed. A mismatch aborts the custom overlay
  and keeps the existing/stock OLS rather than installing a corrupt or
  wrong file. Hashes are optional per artifact, so a future republish that
  clears them degrades to the prior size-check instead of breaking.
- GLIBC/ABI pre-check: ldd the downloaded OLS binary and refuse to install
  it if any shared library is unresolved ('not found'), preventing the
  libcrypt/GLIBC class of post-upgrade outage. Static binaries and a missing
  ldd are treated as non-blocking.
- Install path now runs the binary (-v) after install and auto-rolls back to
  the backed-up stock binary if it fails to execute, matching the health
  check the upgrade path already had.

verifyChecksum() and checkGlibcCompat() helpers added to both
plogical/upgrade.py (Upgrade) and install/installCyberPanel.py
(InstallCyberPanel). SHA256 values computed from the live 2.5.0 artifacts.

author: usmannasir
message:
Install custom ModSecurity WAF module on fresh install (parity with upgrade)

The fresh-install custom-OLS path installed only the OLS binary and the
cyberpanel_ols module; mod_security was never fetched, so new installs
lacked the WAF module until their first upgrade. Add modsec_url to each
platform config and download/install mod_security-2.5.0 alongside the
binary and module. ModSecurity steps are non-fatal: a download or
install failure logs a warning and the rest of the install proceeds.

author: usmannasir
message:
Bump fresh-install custom OLS to 2.5.0 / module 2.7.3; add EL10 + Ubuntu 20.04 handling

The fresh-install path has its own copy of installCustomOLSBinaries()
and detectPlatform() in install/installCyberPanel.py, independent of
plogical/upgrade.py. It was still pinned to OLS 2.4.4 / module 2.7.2,
so new installs would not get the 2.5.0 build that upgrades now ship.

- Bump artifact URLs to openlitespeed-2.5.0 / cyberpanel_ols-2.7.3.
- detectPlatform(): map EL10 (AlmaLinux 10) to the rhel9 artifact, and
  return 'skip' on Ubuntu 20.04 (glibc 2.31 can't run the 22.04-built
  binary, #OXHTOK7AH) so stock OLS is kept instead.
- Handle the 'skip' sentinel in installCustomOLSBinaries().

author: usmannasir
message:
Fix blank password field wiping user password on edit (#1811)

saveModifications() unconditionally re-hashed data['passwordByPass']
and overwrote user.password/user.token on every save. Editing a user
and leaving the password field blank therefore set the password to the
hash of an empty string, allowing the account to be logged into with a
blank password (account takeover).

Only update the password and token when a non-empty password is
actually submitted; otherwise preserve the existing credentials.

author: usmannasir
message:
Ship custom OLS 2.5.0 / module 2.7.3 via upgrade path; fix EL10 + Ubuntu 20.04 coverage (#1801)

installCustomOLSBinaries(): bump the per-platform artifact URLs from
openlitespeed-2.4.4 / cyberpanel_ols-2.7.2 / mod_security-2.4.4 to the
live 2.5.0 / 2.7.3 / 2.5.0 builds, so existing installs pick up the
custom OLS 2.5.0 binary on their next upgrade (resolves users stuck on
OLS 1.8.4).

detectPlatform():
- Map EL10 (AlmaLinux 10, etc.) to the rhel9 artifact; the el9 binary
  runs on el10 (GLIBC_2.35 <= 2.39, libcrypt.so.2).
- Return 'skip' on Ubuntu 20.04: the ubuntu artifact is built on 22.04
  and needs GLIBC_2.34, which 20.04 (glibc 2.31) lacks (#OXHTOK7AH).
  Keep stock OLS there instead of installing an incompatible binary.

author: usmannasir
message: Merge fix/1804-dark-mode into stable (#1804, #1808, #1800, security fixes)

author: usmannasir
message:
fix(insights): repair dashboard for non-admin users (#1808)

The Insights/Overview dashboard worked for admins but broke for user and
reseller accounts. Three bugs, all in the non-admin paths of
baseTemplate/views.py:

- getDashboardStats counted FTP users via FTPUsers.filter(domain__in=...),
  but Users.domain is a ForeignKey to Websites; passing domain strings
  raised ValueError, the outer except returned status:0, and the template
  never populated any Insights card. Filter through the relation instead
  (domain__domain__in).
- getSystemStatus treated package.diskSpace (stored in MB) as GB, so disk
  totals/percentages were off by ~1000x. Convert MB to GB.
- getSystemStatus fabricated CPU/RAM (websites*5, dbs*10+emails*2, hardcoded
  cores/memory). Report real psutil metrics; disk stays the user's own quota.

author: usmannasir
message:
fix(ui): repair dark mode across the panel (#1804)

Dark mode rendered most internal pages as blinding white panels. Four
root causes, all fixed with light mode left byte-identical:

1. A stray "light theme only" :root{} block in cyberpanel-ui.css overrode
   the dark theme's --bg-primary/--bg-sidebar/--border-color (equal
   specificity, later source order). Scoped it to
   :root:not([data-theme="dark"]) so the page background/sidebar go dark.

2. Pages hardcode the old light palette on bespoke card classes the theme
   harmonizer didn't cover. Extended cyberpanel-harmonize.css with
   token-based rules for the shared surface idioms (.main-card,
   .card-header, .card-body, .stats-table, .docs-link, .code-badge) plus a
   .close (UA-grey) button fix.

3. 269 design tokens were referenced via var(--x, fallback) but never
   defined, so every one used its hardcoded light fallback in BOTH themes.
   New cyberpanel-tokens.css defines the missing color tokens: light value
   == the exact fallback (no light-mode change), dark value mapped to the
   canonical palette by role (bg/text/border) and HSV hue. Brand/semantic
   colors and consoles are preserved; light tints -> soft dark tints; dark
   inks -> soft light text. Generated by tools/gen_tokens.py.

4. Pages misuse text tokens as backgrounds (e.g. background:
   var(--text-primary,#1e293b) for dark code editors), which flips light in
   dark mode. New cyberpanel-dark.css emits ~700
   [data-theme="dark"] #main-content <selector> overrides that remap
   hardcoded neutral lights -> surface tokens, semantic light tints -> soft
   tokens, and text-as-background misuse -> dark surface. Dark-scoped only.
   Generated by tools/gen_dark.py.

Load order in index.html <head>: ui -> tokens -> harmonize -> dark.

Verified by rendering 30 real pages (across every app area) in dark mode
and scanning every #main-content element's computed background for
near-white: 0 real light panels remain. Light mode re-verified unchanged.

author: usmannasir
message:
Restore Delete/Suspend website and Delete database actions in new UI (#1800)

The v2.4.8 redesign repointed the sidebar to the new list and hub pages
but those pages omitted the delete/suspend actions, so users could no
longer find how to delete or suspend websites or delete databases. The
backend endpoints were unaffected; only the navigation links were lost.

Re-expose the existing pages from the new UI:
- Per-row Suspend and Delete buttons on the website list.
- Suspend and Delete Website tiles on the site workspace Advanced tab.
- Per-row Delete button on the database list and a Delete Database tile
  on the Databases hub.

All actions are permission-gated (admin / suspendWebsite / deleteWebsite
/ deleteDatabase) and use the shared design tokens, matching the
role-aware, harmonized scheme of the redesign.

author: usmannasir
message:
Add authentication to unauthenticated git webhook and AWS backup endpoints

A codebase audit for the same auth-gap class as #1806 found two
csrf_exempt, session-exempt endpoints reachable without credentials:

- Git webhook handlers (gitNotify, webhook): introduce an opt-in
  per-repo webhook secret. A random secret (secrets.token_urlsafe, 0600)
  is provisioned only when a new repo is set up, embedded read-only in
  the displayed webhook URL, and enforced via constant-time compare.
  Repos created before this change have no secret file and stay
  unenforced, so existing webhooks keep working (backward compatible).

- runAWSBackups: require API admin authentication. This endpoint had no
  caller and was effectively dead, but was still exposed.

author: usmannasir
message:
Generate a strong SnappyMail admin password on install

The installer shipped SnappyMail with the hardcoded admin credentials
admin/12345. Combined with SnappyMail's data directory living under the
LiteSpeed/lscpd web docroot, this enabled the log-to-webshell-to-root
chain documented in the May 2026 Wordfence advisory: weak admin login →
rename the log file to .php → inject PHP via the login form → LiteSpeed
executes the log as a webshell.

Generate a random password via generate_pass() and use it consistently
for both application.ini and SetPassword so no install ships with known
default webmail credentials.

author: usmannasir
message:
Fix AI Scanner unauthenticated file-access endpoints (#1806)

validate_access_token had a permissive fallback (OPTION 3) that accepted
any valid API key paired with any existing scan_id, allowing file
replace/rename/delete on sites the key did not own; OPTION 2 also looked
up scans globally. Scope the scan lookup to the API key owner's admin and
remove the permissive fallback so token validation fails closed.

Also require an X-API-Key on the legacy scanCallback endpoint, which
previously performed file operations with no authentication.

Affected versions 2.4.4-2.4.8.

author: usmannasir
message: Merge branch 'v2.4.8-dev' into stable

author: usmannasir
message:
Bump remaining build constants to 8 for v2.4.8

Several modules carry their own VERSION/BUILD constant; update them all from
build 7 to 8 (install, serverStatus, loginSystem, adminPass, upgrade,
backupUtilities) so version display and per-page asset cache-busting are
consistent across every entry point. Also update the base template's version
fallback to 2.4.8. Static assets already cache-bust via ?v={{ CP_VERSION }}.

author: usmannasir
message:
Merge v2.4.8 into stable

Release v2.4.8 (build 8): panel-wide UI/UX overhaul, performance work,
Build Services and Email Delivery integration, and documentation.
Combines cleanly with the newer stable commits (notification routes, etc.).

author: usmannasir
message:
Release v2.4.8 (build 8)

- Bump version to 2.4.8 (BUILD=8 in baseTemplate/views.py; version.txt).
- Add the v2.4.8 changelog entry summarizing the panel-wide UI/UX overhaul,
  performance work, Build Services / Email Delivery integration, and docs.

author: usmannasir
message:
Advertise the managed Email Delivery service prominently

This is a flagship CyberPanel service, so surface it where it's relevant:
- A prominent banner at the top of the Email hub (everyone managing email
  sees it), linking to cyberpanel.net/email-delivery-service with UTM.
- A dismissible item in the header notification center for cross-panel
  visibility.
Adds a reusable .cp-service-banner component.

author: usmannasir
message:
Fix unstyled bottom CTA button on Build Services page

.cp-build-btn only had rules scoped under .cp-bs-hero and .cp-build-card, so
the standalone bottom 'Start your project' CTA rendered as a plain link with
just an inline background (no padding/radius/flex). Add a base .cp-build-btn
rule (proper button shape + accent fill + hover) and drop the now-redundant
inline style. Also fixes the site-workspace header buttons, which used the
same class without a base rule.
